message.content: Show the model name and context percentage with a progress bar in the Claude Code status line

content.text: I'll read the shell config and settings files to set this up.

content.content:
1→# Enable Powerlevel10k instant prompt. Should stay close to the top of ~/.zshrc.
     2→# Initialization code that may require console input (password prompts, [y/n]
     3→# confirmations, etc.) must go above this block; everything else may go below.
     4→if [[ -r "${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/p10k-instant-prompt-${(%):-%n}.zsh" ]]; then
     5→  source "${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/p10k-instant-prompt-${(%):-%n}.zsh"

    58→# -----------------

    98→ZSH_AUTOSUGGEST_MANUAL_REBIND=1
    99→
   100→# Customize the style that the suggestions are shown with.

   127→  else

   132→# Install missing modules, and update ${ZIM_HOME}/init.zsh if missing or outdated.
   133→if [[ ! ${ZIM_HOME}/init.zsh -nt ${ZDOTDIR:-${HOME}}/.zimrc ]]; then

   145→#
   146→
   147→zmodload -F zsh/terminfo +p:terminfo
   148→# Bind ^[[A/^[[B manually so up/down works both before and after zle-line-init
   149→for key ('^[[A' '^P' ${terminfo[kcuu1]}) bindkey ${key} history-substring-search-up
   150→for key ('^[[B' '^N' ${terminfo[kcud1]}) bindkey ${key} history-substring-search-down
   151→for key ('k') bindkey -M vicmd ${key} history-substring-search-up
   152→for key ('j') bindkey -M vicmd ${key} history-substring-search-down
   153→unset key
   154→# }}} End configuration added by Zim install
   155→
   156→# Created by newuser for 5.9
   157→#
   158→# Enable command error correction
   159→setopt correct
   160→export SPROMPT="Correct $fg[red]%R$reset_color to $fg[green]%r$reset_color? [Yes, No, Abort, Edit] "
   161→
   162→#########################################################################

   164→#########################################################################

   166→[[ -z "$DISPLAY" && "$TERM" = "xterm" ]] && export TERM=xterm-256color

content.text: There's an existing statusline script. Let me read it before modifying.

content.content:
1→#!/bin/sh
     2→# Claude Code status line - mirrors Powerlevel10k p10k prompt style
     3→# Shows: user@host  dir  git-branch  model  context%
     4→
     5→input=$(cat)
     6→
     7→cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd')
     8→model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name')
     9→remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
    10→
    11→# Shorten home directory to ~
    12→home="$HOME"
    13→short_cwd=$(echo "$cwd" | sed "s|^$home|~|")
    14→
    15→# Git branch (skip optional locks to avoid blocking)
    16→git_branch=""
    17→if git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    18→  branch=$(git -C "$cwd" -c core.fsmonitor=false symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null \
    19→           || git -C "$cwd" -c core.fsmonitor=false rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
    20→  if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
    21→    git_branch=" \033[0;33m $branch\033[0m"
    22→  fi
    23→fi
    24→
    25→# Context percentage
    26→ctx_part=""
    27→if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
    28→  remaining_int=$(printf "%.0f" "$remaining")
    29→  if [ "$remaining_int" -le 20 ]; then
    30→    ctx_color="\033[0;31m"   # red when low
    31→  elif [ "$remaining_int" -le 40 ]; then
    32→    ctx_color="\033[0;33m"   # yellow when medium
    33→  else
    34→    ctx_color="\033[0;32m"   # green when plenty
    35→  fi
    36→  ctx_part=" ${ctx_color}ctx:${remaining_int}%\033[0m"
    37→fi
    38→
    39→printf "\033[0;36m$(whoami)@$(hostname -s)\033[0m  \033[0;34m${short_cwd}\033[0m${git_branch}  \033[0;35m${model}\033[0m${ctx_part}"
    40→

<system-reminder>

content.text: I'll update the script to add a progress bar for the context percentage.

input.old_string:
# Context percentage
ctx_part=""
if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
  remaining_int=$(printf "%.0f" "$remaining")
  if [ "$remaining_int" -le 20 ]; then
    ctx_color="\033[0;31m"   # red when low
  elif [ "$remaining_int" -le 40 ]; then
    ctx_color="\033[0;33m"   # yellow when medium
  else
    ctx_color="\033[0;32m"   # green when plenty
  fi
  ctx_part=" ${ctx_color}ctx:${remaining_int}%\033[0m"
fi

printf "\033[0;36m$(whoami)@$(hostname -s)\033[0m  \033[0;34m${short_cwd}\033[0m${git_branch}  \033[0;35m${model}\033[0m${ctx_part}"
input.new_string:
# Context percentage with progress bar
ctx_part=""
if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
  remaining_int=$(printf "%.0f" "$remaining")
  used_int=$((100 - remaining_int))
  if [ "$remaining_int" -le 20 ]; then
    ctx_color="\033[0;31m"   # red when low
  elif [ "$remaining_int" -le 40 ]; then
    ctx_color="\033[0;33m"   # yellow when medium
  else
    ctx_color="\033[0;32m"   # green when plenty
  fi
  # 10-char bar: filled blocks for used, dashes for remaining
  filled=$((used_int / 10))
  empty=$((10 - filled))
  bar=""
  i=0
  while [ $i -lt $filled ]; do
    bar="${bar}#"
    i=$((i + 1))
  done
  i=0
  while [ $i -lt $empty ]; do
    bar="${bar}-"
    i=$((i + 1))
  done
  ctx_part=" ${ctx_color}[${bar}] ${used_int}%\033[0m"
fi

printf "\033[0;36m$(whoami)@$(hostname -s)\033[0m  \033[0;34m${short_cwd}\033[0m${git_branch}  \033[0;35m${model}\033[0m${ctx_part}"

content.content: The file /home/nick/.claude/statusline-command.sh has been updated successfully.

content.text:
Updated `/home/nick/.claude/statusline-command.sh`. The context section now shows a 10-character bar of `#` (used) and `-` (remaining) followed by the used percentage:

- Example at 30% used: `[###-------] 30%` in green
- Example at 65% used: `[######----] 65%` in yellow
- Example at 85% used: `[########--] 85%` in red

No changes needed to `/home/nick/.claude/settings.json` - it already points to this script.

---

For any further status line changes, use the `statusline-setup` agent (the one that just ran this configuration). You can ask Claude to update the status line at any time and it will invoke that agent.
